musaiumGates.noUnicodeEmoji: PASS — grep on new files (useCollapsibleTopBar.ts, CollapsibleTopBar.tsx) yields no unicode emoji. Existing arrow chars (80↓ / 40↑) in JSDoc comments are reused from A2 doc style, not user-visible.
musaiumGates.noFeatureFlag: PASS — no `*_ENABLED` introduced. The `collapsed` prop is a layout state controlled by scroll, not a runtime toggle. Doctrine `feedback_no_feature_flags_prelaunch` respected.
musaiumGates.buryDeadCode: PASS — `<ChatHeader>` remains the live header (consumed by `<CollapsibleTopBar>`). No DEPRECATED markers, no zombie stub, no orphan import. The screen replaces `<ChatHeader>` with `<CollapsibleTopBar>` cleanly (one import flipped, JSX wrapper swapped).
musaiumGates.noNewI18nKeys: PASS — `git diff HEAD -- museum-frontend/shared/locales/` shows no addition tied to A4 (existing locale-touch comes from prior in-flight features). `<CollapsibleTopBar>` only forwards labels (R13).
musaiumGates.noNewDeps: PASS — `git diff HEAD -- museum-frontend/package.json` empty. `Animated` is core RN, `useReducedMotion` is the existing `shared/ui/hooks/useReducedMotion.ts`. NFR7 respected.

lint.output: eslint . --max-warnings=0 && tsc --noEmit → no errors, no warnings

tests.summary: Test Suites: 229 passed, 229 total ; Tests: 2372 passed, 2372 total ; Snapshots: 4 passed
tests.delta: +32 vs baseline 2340 — matches A4 spec (T5.2 + tracking.md A4 row : 14 helper tests + 18 wrapper/ChatHeader tests = 32)
tests.workerLeakNote: Jest emits one 'A worker process has failed to exit gracefully' warning at the end (unchanged baseline behaviour — already present pre-A4). Not introduced by A4. Documented as known noise.

scopedTests.note: Repo's test runner ignores --testPathPattern (custom node-test compiler config), but the targeted suites both appear in the PASS list : `__tests__/features/chat/useCollapsibleTopBar.test.tsx` PASS and `__tests__/components/CollapsibleTopBar.test.tsx` PASS (14 + 18 = 32 cases counted via `grep -cE '^\s+it\('`).

correctness.rationale: All 24 EARS (R1-R24) and 20 ACs (AC1-AC20) satisfied by the diff. Helper hysteresis verified literally: `if (previousCollapsed) return scrollY >= 40; return scrollY >= 80;` matches R1-R4 boundary semantics (R3 says >=40 stays collapsed, R4 says <40 re-expands ; the impl returns `scrollY >= 40` which is true at 40 → matches R3 ; false at 39 → matches R4 — AC6/AC7 explicit). Wrapper Animated.View with `useNativeDriver: false`, 200ms duration, reduced-motion snap via `setValue` (R11/R12). `<ChatHeader>` collapsed prop drives `fontSize.base` + ExpertiseBadge gate (R17/R18). AI badge stays rendered in both states (R10/R19 — EU AI Act Art.50). Screen wires the new state via shared `onListScroll` callback (R22/R23). Zero BE touched (R24).
correctness.evidence: useCollapsibleTopBar.ts:26-31 (helper) ; CollapsibleTopBar.tsx:49-69 (effect snap/timing) ; ChatHeader.tsx:54-63,82 (title style + badge gate) ; [sessionId].tsx working-tree diff lines +19, +106-110, +122, +390, +403 (wire-up).
minorNotes: Mount-time `Animated.Value(EXPANDED_HEIGHT)` — when the screen mounts with `collapsed={true}` (which never happens at scrollY=0 but is theoretically possible if state is pre-seeded), the effect runs synchronously and `setValue` snaps before paint when reducedMotion=true, else animates 200ms. Acceptable per spec §2.3 (the inline comment in CollapsibleTopBar.tsx:44-46 calls this out honestly).

scope-fidelity.rationale: ZERO new dep (NFR7), ZERO new DS token (R/§2.6 reuses fontSize.base + fontSize['2xl']), ZERO new i18n key (R13/NFR3), ZERO BE change (R24), ZERO telemetry (NFR4), ZERO feature flag (NFR11), ZERO Expo Router stack touch (§0.2 + §1.5). Diff strictly limited to : 2 new FE files (helper + wrapper), 2 new test files, 1 ChatHeader.tsx prop addition, 1 [sessionId].tsx wire-up (import swap + state + onScroll dispatch + JSX swap).
scope-fidelity.evidence: git status shows only the 5 expected mutations (+2 helper/wrapper, +2 tests, ChatHeader.tsx mod, [sessionId].tsx mod, tracking.md status flip). Locale files are dirty but the diff is unrelated to A4 (carried over from sibling in-flight features). No package.json delta.

kiss-dry-hexagonal.rationale: Helper isolated in `application/` layer mirrors the A2 sibling (`useArtworkHero.ts` exports `deriveHeroCollapsed`) for cross-feature consistency. Wrapper is a thin React.memo over `<ChatHeader>` (39 SLOC pure code). `<ChatHeader>` backward-compat via default `collapsed = false`. `ChatHeaderProps` exported so the wrapper can `extends ChatHeaderProps` — clean prop-drilling, no global store. The `useMemo(() => new Animated.Value(EXPANDED_HEIGHT), [])` instead of `useRef(new Animated.Value(...)).current` is a defensible alternative — eliminates the React-Compiler / react-hooks-rules friction documented inline (CollapsibleTopBar.tsx:42-46). Honest trade-off, justified comment, no eslint-disable.
minorNotes: `useMemo(() => new Animated.Value(...), [])` is functionally equivalent to `useRef(new Animated.Value(...)).current` (both produce a single stable instance for the component lifetime). Spec §2.3 used the `useRef` form ; impl swapped to `useMemo` with an inline justification (compiler/lint friction). Drift is sub-cosmetic — both patterns are idiomatic in the codebase and the test suite passes 32/32. Listed as nice-to-have N1.

`StyleSheet.create({ shell: { overflow: 'hidden' } })` is a 1-key stylesheet — could be inlined but the dedicated stylesheet is consistent with the codebase convention (avoids the `react-native/no-inline-styles` lint warning class).

a11y-design-system.rationale: All accessibility labels on ChatHeader preserved (`a11y.chat.close`, `visitSummary.visitSummary`, `chat.audio_mode_off/on`, `voice.disclosure.badgeA11y`). `accessibilityRole='button'` preserved on each Pressable. AI badge testID `ai-disclosure-badge` preserved in collapsed (red-test AC10 asserts both states render it). DS tokens respected — `fontSize.base` (collapsed) / `fontSize['2xl']` (expanded), `space['9']` for 36dp buttons, `radius.full`, `semantic.input.borderWidth`. `useReducedMotion()` honoured — snap via `setValue` (R12/WCAG 2.3.3). The 44dp collapsed height is ≥ iOS HIG 32dp minimum for action bars. ExpertiseBadge is removed from the React tree when collapsed → VoiceOver does not read it (consistent with R18 + §0.6 ; trade-off documented spec §0.6).
a11y-design-system.evidence: ChatHeader.tsx:71-72,95-98,114,127 (a11y labels) ; ChatHeader.tsx:167-169 (DS token `fontSize.base`) ; CollapsibleTopBar.tsx:54-57 (reduced-motion snap).
minorNotes: No explicit `accessibilityRole='header'` set on the wrapper — `<ChatHeader>` doesn't carry one at baseline either, so this is unchanged behaviour (out of A4 scope ; would be a `<ChatHeader>` follow-up). Listed for awareness, not a finding.

security-honesty.rationale: Zero PII transit, zero telemetry, zero new persistence (NFR4 ephemeral screen-local state). Zero `as any`, zero `eslint-disable` added (lint exit 0 with `--max-warnings=0`). No TODO/FIXME left in the new files (grep on both confirms). JSDoc honestly documents the trade-off on `useMemo` vs `useRef` (CollapsibleTopBar.tsx:42-46) and the `useNativeDriver: false` constraint (CollapsibleTopBar.tsx:11-14). The animation `.stop()` cleanup on effect teardown is present (CollapsibleTopBar.tsx:66-68) — prevents in-flight animation leak when `collapsed` flips fast. The Jest worker-leak warning is pre-existing baseline noise (not introduced by A4), so the report is honest about that.
security-honesty.evidence: useCollapsibleTopBar.ts ; CollapsibleTopBar.tsx:66-68 (animation cleanup) ; lint exit code 0 verified.
minorNotes: Spec §2.7 claimed `Animated useNativeDriver: false acceptable car single transition ponctuelle 200ms` — impl honours this and the comment on CollapsibleTopBar.tsx:11-14 restates it without hand-waving.

niceToHave.title: Spec §2.3 uses `useRef(new Animated.Value(...)).current` ; impl uses `useMemo(() => new Animated.Value(...), [])`
niceToHave.evidence: Both patterns yield a single stable `Animated.Value` instance for the component lifetime — semantically equivalent for this purpose. Impl inline comment (lines 42-46) documents the choice : 'created once via stable empty-deps useMemo so React Compiler / react-hooks rules don't flag a ref read during render'. The 32 A4 tests + lint --max-warnings=0 all pass with this form.
niceToHave.honest-tradeoff: `useRef` would have matched the spec literally ; `useMemo` is idiomatic for React 19 / React Compiler era code (the codebase uses both patterns interchangeably — see e.g. existing `Animated.Value` usages). Drift is sub-cosmetic, justification documented in code. Acceptable.
niceToHave.remediation: Optional : swap to `useRef(new Animated.Value(EXPANDED_HEIGHT)).current` to match spec verbatim. Or amend spec §2.3 to note both forms acceptable. No functional impact.

niceToHave.title: If the wrapper ever mounts with `collapsed={true}` it briefly renders at 80dp before the effect snaps it to 44dp (only matters when reducedMotion=false, where the user sees a 200ms collapse anim)
niceToHave.evidence: `Animated.Value(EXPANDED_HEIGHT)` is the constructor seed regardless of the initial `collapsed` value. The effect on first mount transitions to the correct target. When `reducedMotion=true` the snap is via `setValue` (instantaneous, before next paint in most cases). When `reducedMotion=false`, the user would see a one-time collapse animation 80→44dp on first mount with collapsed=true.
niceToHave.spec-ref: A4.md §2.3 ; in practice the screen always mounts with `topBarCollapsed=false` (useState default) so this edge never fires.
niceToHave.honest-tradeoff: In production the screen-local state `topBarCollapsed` defaults to `false` (line +106 of the screen diff), so this code path is never exercised at mount — the only flips happen after `onListScroll` updates state. Acceptable. The inline comment (CollapsibleTopBar.tsx:44-46) calls this out honestly.
niceToHave.remediation: Optional follow-up V1.1 if a future feature ever pre-seeds collapsed=true at mount : seed the `Animated.Value` with `collapsed ? COLLAPSED_HEIGHT : EXPANDED_HEIGHT`. Currently un-needed.

niceToHave.title: Jest emits 'A worker process has failed to exit gracefully' at the end of `npm test`
niceToHave.evidence: Warning appears at the tail of the full test run. Tests still report exit 0 and 2372/2372 PASS. Same warning is present in prior A1/A2/A3 review runs per existing review JSONs.
niceToHave.spec-ref: Not an A4 finding — pre-existing baseline noise.
niceToHave.honest-tradeoff: Not introduced by A4. The new CollapsibleTopBar test does not start any timer or worker (only render/fireEvent) — the leak is elsewhere in the suite. Documented here so the build-verification claim is honest.
niceToHave.remediation: Out of A4 scope. Suggest a follow-up investigation with `--detectOpenHandles` on the full suite, owned by the test-infra track.

T1-usememo-over-useref.evidence: Both yield identical lifetime semantics for `Animated.Value`. Impl inline comment honestly states the reason (React Compiler / react-hooks rule friction). Lint exit 0 + 32/32 A4 tests pass. See finding N1.

T2-mount-time-value-seed.evidence: Always mounted with `topBarCollapsed=false` in the current screen — edge case is theoretical only. Code path documented. See finding N2.

T3-animated-rn-not-reanimated.evidence: Spec §0.4 explicitly chose Animated RN over Reanimated for KISS (single property height transition, no gesture, ponctuelle). Impl matches. `useNativeDriver: false` honest about layout-prop constraint. Driver choice documented in code header (lines 11-14).

T4-shared-onlistscroll-source.evidence: Spec §1.4 R23 mandated 'same `onListScroll` callback updates BOTH via `deriveHeroCollapsed` and `deriveTopBarCollapsed`'. Impl ([sessionId].tsx:120-123 working-tree diff) dispatches both setState calls inside the one useCallback. Re-renders are gated by hysteresis crossings — not per-scroll-frame. Perf NFR1 respected.

T5-jest-worker-leak-disclosure.evidence: Pre-existing baseline noise, not introduced by A4. Report flags it honestly under finding N3 instead of glossing over it (UFR-013 truth-telling).

gates.musaium-doctrine: PASS — 5/5 (no emoji, no flag, no dead code, no new i18n, no new deps)
gates.honesty: PASS — known caveats (Jest worker leak, useMemo drift, mount-time seed) reported explicitly

recommandation.rationale: weightedMean 94 ≥ 85 threshold. Zero blocker, zero important. 3 nice-to-haves are all sub-cosmetic / pre-existing / theoretical edge cases — none warrant a return to green. Cohérent avec A1/A2/A3 review patterns (also approved with N1-N3 nice-to-haves only). A4 can flip `review → done` in tracking.md.
recommandation.merge-readiness: Ready. Lint + tests + ESLint discipline + Musaium gates all green. No follow-up V1 work required.
